articles.title: ‘Developers should have free rein’
articles.summary: Binyamin Appelbaum of The New York Times discusses a city that may have resolved its housing crisis. He characterizes Vancouver as a location that has failed significantly in the effort to build sufficient housing. This opinion piece is featured in a collection of daily editorials.
articles.description: Opinion, comment and editorials of the day
articles.author: theweek@futurenet.com (Justin Klawans, The Week US) , Justin Klawans, The Week US
articles.cleanedText: ‘One city might have just cracked the housing crisis’ Binyamin Appelbaum at The New York Times Vancouver is the “place that has failed most spectacularly in the basic work of building enough housin…

articles.title: Tim Hodgson: A divided Canada cannot build what the world needs
articles.summary: Tim Hodgson argues that Canada is currently experiencing a historical moment where the need for national unity is clear. He suggests that amidst global uncertainty, successful countries will be those that focus on building rather than internal conflict.
articles.description: In this time of global uncertainty, the countries that succeed will not be the ones that fight amongst themselves. They will be the ones that build
articles.author: Special to Financial Post
articles.cleanedText: There are times in a countrys history when the need for unity becomes clear for all to see. Right now, Canada is living through one of those moments. THIS CONTENT IS RESERVED FOR SUBSCRIBERS ONLY S…

articles.title: European Stocks Gain on Iran-US Deal, Close Off Record High
articles.summary: European Stocks Gain on Iran-US Deal, Close Off Record High. European stocks closed modestly higher Monday, ceding most of their earlier gains sparked by an interim US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: The 'Super' El Niño Has Arrived. Here's How It Might Affect the World's Weather and Economy
articles.summary: The 'Super' El Niño Has Arrived. Here's How It Might Affect the World's Weather and Economy. The naturally occurring climate pattern, characterized by warm surface water in the Pacific Ocean, that has just started could be one of the strongest ever recorded, according to experts

articles.title: US Oil Reserve Hits 43-Year Low as Trump Aims to Tame Gas Prices
articles.summary: US Oil Reserve Hits 43-Year Low as Trump Aims to Tame Gas Prices. The US’s emergency supply of crude oil has fallen to its lowest level since 1983 as the Trump administration moves toward completing its plan to release 172 million barrels to ease surging fuel prices spurred by the war in Iran.
